data.title: Russia plans to lower the barriers to cryptocurrency investment and expand the scope of legal investors.
data.description: Russian central banks are drafting new regulations for cryptocurrency, potentially allowing previously ineligible investors to purchase crypto assets. On November 26, Russian Deputy Finance Minister Ivan Chebeskov revealed that the Ministry of Finance and the central bank are planning to remove the current strict regulations limiting participation in the cryptocurrency market to "highly qualified" investors, and propose establishing a tiered access mechanism to broaden the scope of legitimate investors. Current regulations require individual investors to hold at least 100 million rubles in bank deposits and securities, and to have verifiable income exceeding 50 million rubles in the past year to qualify for "highly qualified" certification. (Bloomberg)

data.title: About an hour ago, 93,793 FLOKS were deposited into the CEX, worth approximately $540,000.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, a total of 93,793 FLOKS tokens, worth approximately $540,000, were deposited into CEX in the past hour. Another wallet currently holds approximately $720,000 worth of FOLKS tokens, which may also be deposited into CEX.

data.title: Russia Considers New Cryptocurrency Regulations to Broaden Investor Access
data.description: According to Odaily, the Russian central bank is developing a new regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies that may allow previously ineligible investors to purchase digital assets. On November 26, Russian Deputy Finance Minister Ivan Chebeskov disclosed that the Ministry of Finance and the central bank are planning to remove the current stringent rules that restrict cryptocurrency market participation to 'highly qualified' investors. The proposed changes aim to establish a tiered access system to expand the range of legitimate investors. Under the existing regulations, individual investors must have at least 100 million rubles in bank deposits and securities, and demonstrate an annual income exceeding 50 million rubles to qualify as 'highly qualified' investors.

data.title: XRP just flashed set up for major price rebound; Is 3 next?
data.description:
A ray of hope is emerging for XRP, which may help the token reclaim the $2 resistance zone after several days of trading below that level. In this context, the token is flashing a contrarian signal that has historically preceded strong upside moves, as market sentiment on social media turns decisively negative while price action stabilizes. Data tracking social media commentary shows that negative sentiment is dominating discussions around the token, a condition that has repeatedly coincided with local market bottoms rather than sustained declines, according to insights shared by Santiment on December 23. XRP social sentiment
source: https://finbold.com/xrp-just-flashed-set-up-for-major-price-rebound-is-3-next/

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin's short-term support level may be in the $84,000 to $84,500 range.
data.description: Analyst Omkar Godbole stated that Bitcoin's short-term support level is currently in the $84,000 to $84,500 range. A break below this level could lead to a test of the November low of $80,000. The analysis indicates that only a break above this trendline resistance could allow Bitcoin to resume its upward trend, potentially targeting $100,000. Bitcoin's price was capped on December 23rd by the downtrend line from its all-time high of $126,000, failing to break the $90,000 mark and continuing its downward trend from the fourth quarter. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Elliptic: Two major Chinese-language encrypted fraud platforms on Telegram reportedly handle nearly $2 billion in illicit funds monthly.
data.description: A recent analysis by cryptocurrency tracking firm Elliptic shows that the predominantly Chinese-language cryptocurrency black market ecosystem on Telegram has reached an all-time high. Two major platforms built on Telegram facilitate nearly $2 billion monthly in money laundering and fraud-related transactions, including money laundering services, stolen data, fake investment websites, AI deepfake tools, and human trafficking. Researchers point out that these markets have surpassed dark web platforms like AlphaBay and Hydra to become the largest illicit online cryptocurrency market in history. (Wired)

data.title: Record Growth in Chinese-Language Crypto Black Market on Telegram
data.description: According to Odaily, recent analysis by cryptocurrency tracking firm Elliptic reveals that the Chinese-language crypto black market on Telegram has reached unprecedented levels. Two major platforms built on Telegram are facilitating nearly $2 billion in monthly transactions related to money laundering and fraudulent activities. These include services for laundering money, stolen data, fake investment websites, AI deepfake tools, and human trafficking. Researchers have noted that these markets have surpassed dark web platforms like AlphaBay and Hydra, becoming the largest illegal online crypto markets in history.

data.title: Ontario Healthcare Pension Fund Invests in Bitcoin Treasury Firm
data.description: According to Odaily, the Ontario Healthcare Pension Fund, with assets totaling $90 billion, has acquired 14.8 million shares of Bitcoin Treasury company Strive (ASST), valued at $13 million.

data.title: Wintermute: Bitcoin market share rebounds, funds flow back to mainstream cryptocurrencies
data.description: Wintermute released its market report today, stating that as the holidays approach, the market structure continues to narrow, and Bitcoin's market share has risen again. From Wintermute's observation of internal fund flow data, the following findings can be made: Buying power is once again stronger than selling power in major cryptocurrencies; BTC's buying advantage is longer-lasting and more stable, and ETH also shows greater signs of increased buying at the end of the year; institutional funds have been a consistent source of buying since the summer; retail investors are starting to rotate back from altcoins to major cryptocurrencies; this retail rotation aligns with market consensus: BTC needs to lead the rally first, followed by altcoin rotation.

data.title: Franklin Templeton's XRP Spot ETF Holdings Surpass 100 Million
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Franklin Templeton has updated the holdings data for its XRP spot ETF. As of December 22, the ETF's XRP holdings have exceeded 100 million, reaching 101,552,283.62 XRP. The market value of these holdings is approximately $192,683,271.89. Additionally, the ETF's total net asset value stands at $183.41 million, with 8,900,000 shares currently in circulation.

data.title: Ontario's pension fund buys $13 million worth of Strive stock.
data.description: The Ontario Senior Care (OSC) pension fund, with assets totaling $90 billion, has purchased 14.8 million shares of Bitcoin crypto treasury (DAT) company Strive (ASST), worth $13 million. (BitcoinTreasuries)

data.title: Franklin Templeton XRP spot ETF holdings surpass 100 million XRP for the first time.
data.description: Franklin Templeton has officially updated the holdings data for its XRP spot ETF. As of December 22, the ETF's XRP holdings have surpassed 100 million for the first time, reaching 101,552,283.62 XRP (custodied by Coinbase Custody), with a market value of $192,683,271.89. Furthermore, the ETF's total net asset value is currently $183.41 million, and the number of outstanding shares is 8,900,000.

data.title: Hyperscale Data increased its Bitcoin holdings to 515 and allocated $30.5 million to continue purchasing.
data.description: Hyperscale Data, a NYSE American company, announced it will allocate approximately $76 million to its Bitcoin treasury, representing about 100.75% of the company's market capitalization. Its wholly-owned subsidiary, Sentinum, currently holds 514.9655 bitcoins (including 440.2341 bitcoins acquired on the open market and approximately 74.7313 bitcoins from its Bitcoin mining operations). The company has also allocated $30.5 million in cash to continue purchasing bitcoins on the open market, aiming to reach a bitcoin value of $100 million on its balance sheet. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Analysis: Reduced market liquidity during the Christmas holidays may limit Bitcoin's significant price increase.
data.description: Bitcoin's price failed to hold above $90,000 for an extended period due to factors including reduced liquidity from the Christmas holidays, a surge in precious metal prices, and an upcoming record number of options expirations. These factors collectively impacted the cryptocurrency market's price movements. Approximately 300,000 Bitcoin options contracts (with a notional value of about $23.7 billion) expired on Friday, double the number from the same period last year, with positions concentrated around the $85,000 and $100,000 strike prices. Timothy Misir, Head of Research at BRN, stated that the market tone remains defensive. Rebounds lack follow-through momentum, while sell-offs, though small in scale, have been prolonged. (The Block)

data.title: XRP faces far more negative social media commentary than average
data.description:
XRP is drawing unusually high levels of negative chatter on social media, and according to some market watchers, doubts that the token’s price will rise have historically led to rebounds. Crypto market sentiment analytics firm Santiment posted on X that sentiment around the token has slipped deeply into negative territory, but episodes of pessimism are […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/xrp-negative-social-media-commentary/
